raw_consumer_statement:
On or about XX/XX/XXXX, I dined out in XXXX, Florida, with a friend, and we split the bill. My share, including tip, should have been approximately {$37.00}. However, I was charged over {$96.00}. Initially, I attempted to resolve the issue with the merchant, but to no avail. Subsequently, I contacted your dispute department and created a case # XXXX. At that time, they requested that I provide the receipt. I explained that I had lost it during my travel. They assured me that they would conduct an investigation and notify me of the outcome. 

A few weeks later, I received a letter stating that, after an investigation, they were unable to take any further action and had closed the case. 
when I inquired as to why the case was closed they stated it was because I didn't provide the receipt. I told them from the start I had lost it. If they knew that then what was the investigation. I then asked them to ask the merchant for their receipt, the one with my authorization signature, and that would clearly show I was grossly over charged. they said they couldn't and refused to answer why they couldn't ask for the receipt. 

if this had been hundreds or thousands of dollars I would have been unprotected.
